sourceMovie.title: Rust
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Rust works as a western movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The western and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Open Range
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rust clicked for you, Open Range (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in western and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Rust?
faq.answer: Try Open Range, Old Henry, Forsaken—each shares DNA with Rust while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Rust?
faq.answer: Rust (2025) is best described as Western, Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Rust worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this western lane.
